textPlain: This is an amazing timeline. I still remember the day redstone was added to Minecraft. I spent the entire evening and many days afterwards on the forum brainstorming how to implement various things. I think I had one of the first if not the first T flip flop, it "took an entire room" and was slow. It has been crazy watching things get compacted, repeaters getting added, pistons, comparators. I remember when BUDs got discovered and then eventually just added as a block.Now* we have an entire HDL.I honestly stopped keeping track of things around 2012 so I am completely lost looking at modern redstone contraptions.*8 years ago

In case anyone needs a minimal CPU implementation in 65 lines of Verilog:
https://people.csail.mit.edu/ebakke/fic/
https://people.csail.mit.edu/ebakke/fic/code/Fic.v(I wonder if it would convert cleanly to a redstone circuit...)

Related:Verilog to Minecraft Redstone Synthesizer - https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=25195802 - Nov 2020 (12 comments)(Reposts are fine after a year or so! https://news.ycombinator.com/newsfaq.html)

Super cool project :)
Just the right level of, objectively useless - but really fun!
Optimizing passes for this would be interesting.Describing a flip flop as a villager minecart with some number of NaN minecarts beside it seems challenging to pick when to use it vs a copper bulb.
Minecraft circuits can't have feedback? That's pretty sad:(

> A 2-bit 7-segment display decoder in action (the display itself was not generated by MinecraftHDL)Lame!(/s)
I did this vanilla Minecraft(1.12?), including the display itself.
This compiler does not support sequential logic, meaning no flip flops/registers.
They can. It's just that this specific program is not capable of translating it.
